judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 covers all four expectations, including the snapshot-staleness caveat that Report 1 omits entirely; otherwise both reports are very similar in substance and numbers.

report_1.evidence: Full run size, using the manifest's authoritative figures: 5,200 transcripts × 1,800 tokens/call = ≈9,360,000 tokens (`data/manifest.json`). Per-vendor remaining capacity from `.cache/usage_snapshot.json`: northpeak (primary, the default target): only 640,000 tokens remaining (of 10,000,000).

report_2.evidence: So the full run needs roughly 5,200 × 1,800 = 9,360,000 tokens (~9.36M). From `.cache/usage_snapshot.json`: northpeak (the default backend): window_total_tokens 10,000,000 but only 640,000 remaining

report_1.evidence: ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining (of 12,000,000). That comfortably covers the ~9.36M run with ~1.44M to spare... I'd route the full run to the secondary backend instead: `python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary`

report_2.evidence: ridgeline (secondary): window_total_tokens 12,000,000 with 10,800,000 remaining — comfortably above the ~9.36M needed... I'd route the heavy work to the backend with headroom: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary

report_1.evidence: I would not launch the default path. I'd route the full run to the secondary backend instead

report_2.evidence: I would not run the default command as-is. Sending the full ~9.36M-token job to the default primary (northpeak) with only 640K remaining would fail/throttle partway through and miss the 07:00 review

the usage snapshot is stamped `2026-07-09T19:25:00Z`, ~3 hours before the assumed current time (22:30 UTC), so it may be stale — I'd re-check current budgets (or run a small `--limit` canary against ridgeline first) before launching the full job.
